session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic.
I need to understand how the @no_repo_required pattern works in erk. Specifically:
1. How does the CLI framework know that a command can be run without a repo? Is there a `no_repo_required` marker on the Click command, or a list somewhere? 2. How does `pr dispatch` get registered to work without a repo? 3. Search for any `NO_REPO` or `no_repo` string in `src/erk/cli/cli.py` and related context setup code 4. Check `src/erk/cli/core.py` for how ErkContext is constructed and whether there's something controlling whether NoRepoSentinel is used
Provide the full answer with code references.
session.assistant: I'll help you understand the `@no_repo_required` pattern in erk. Let me start by checking the documentation and then searching the codebase systematically.
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

session.assistant: Great! There's already documentation on this. Let me read the existing documentation:
session.tool_result: EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory, read

1→--- 2→title: No-Repo Infrastructure 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a command that works outside git repositories" 5→ - "using NoRepoSentinel or @no_repo_required" 6→ - "implementing remote dispatch without local repo context" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "accessing ctx.git or ctx.repo without checking for NoRepoSentinel" 9→ warning: "Commands decorated with @no_repo_required may have sentinel gateways. Check isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) before repo operations." 10→--- 11→ 12→# No-Repo Infrastructure 13→ 14→Erk commands normally require a git repository context. The no-repo infrastructure allows specific commands to run outside repositories by providing sentinel gateways that raise user-friendly errors instead of crashing. 15→ 16→## NoRepoSentinel Pattern 17→ 18→Sentinel gateways implement the same ABC interfaces as real gateways but raise `NoRepoError` with guidance when called. This means commands can be wired up normally — the error only fires if the command actually tries to use repo-dependent functionality. 19→ 20→## `@no_repo_required` Decorator 21→ 22→Commands decorated with `@no_repo_required` skip repository detection during context construction. Instead of failing at startup when no repo is found, the context is built with sentinel gateways for repo-dependent services. 23→ 24→## RemoteGitHub Consolidation 25→ 26→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py --> 27→ 28→The one-shot dispatch command demonstrates the full no-repo pattern. It supports both local repo mode and remote-only mode via `--repo owner/name`: 29→ 30→### Context Injection Pattern
... [25 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Let me read the plan and then look at the actual implementation:
session.tool_result:
1→# Plan: Convert `erk launch` to @no_repo_required with --repo flag 2→ 3→Part of Objective #8832, Node 3.3 (launch-remote) 4→ 5→## Context 6→ 7→The `erk launch` command dispatches GitHub Actions workflows (pr-rebase, pr-address, pr-rewrite, learn, one-shot). It currently requires a local git repo for all operations, even though the actual work is done remotely via GitHub Actions. This prevents using `erk launch` from machines without a local clone (e.g., remote dispatch from a phone, CI, or a different project directory). 8→ 9→The goal is to add a `--repo owner/repo` flag so `erk launch` can work without a local git repository, following the same unified pattern established in node 3.2 (pr dispatch). 10→ 11→## Approach 12→ 13→### 1. Add `get_pr` to RemoteGitHub gateway 14→ 15→The launch command needs PR-specific fields (head_ref_name, base_ref_name, state) that `get_issue()` doesn't provide. Add a `get_pr` method to the RemoteGitHub ABC calling `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}`. 16→ 17→**New file: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/types.py`** 18→- `RemotePRInfo` frozen dataclass: number, title, state, url, head_ref_name, base_ref_name, owner, repo, labels 19→- `RemotePRNotFound` frozen dataclass: pr_number 20→ 21→**Modify: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py`** 22→- Add abstract `get_pr(*, owner, repo, number) -> RemotePRInfo | RemotePRNotFound` 23→ 24→**Modify: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/real.py`** 25→- Implement via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}` 26→- Map state: API returns lowercase "open"/"closed" + `merged` bool → "OPEN"/"CLOSED"/"MERGED" 27→ 28→**Modify: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/fake.py`** 29→- Add `prs: dict[int, RemotePRInfo]` constructor parameter 30→- Implement `get_pr` returning from dict or `RemotePRNotFound`
... [91 lines omitted] ...

Run 'erk init' to create it.") 20→ 21→ result = discover_repo_or_sentinel(start, ctx.global_config.erk_root, ctx.git) 22→ if isinstance(result, RepoContext): 23→ return result 24→ raise FileNotFoundError(result.message) 25→ 26→ 27→def worktree_path_for(worktrees_dir: Path, name: str) -> Path: 28→ """Return the absolute path for a named worktree within worktrees directory. 29→ 30→ Note: Does not handle 'root' as a special case. 1→"""Command to dispatch a task for fully autonomous remote execution. 2→ 3→No local planning — the remote Claude explores the codebase, plans the change, 4→implements it, and creates a PR. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk one-shot "fix the import in config.py" 8→ erk one-shot --file prompt.md 9→ erk one-shot "add type hints to utils.py" --model opus 10→ erk one-shot "fix the typo in README.md" --dry-run 11→ erk one-shot "rename issue_number to plan_number in impl_init.py" --plan-only 12→ erk one-shot "fix config bug" --repo owner/repo 13→""" 14→ 15→from pathlib import Path 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk.cli.commands.implement_shared import normalize_model_name 20→from erk.cli.commands.one_shot_remote_dispatch import ( 21→ OneShotDispatchParams, 22→ dispatch_one_shot_remote, 23→) 24→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 25→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError 26→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel 27→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 28→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.real import RealRemoteGitHub 29→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 30→

100→ click.echo(f"{indent}• {item_text}", file=sys.stderr) 101→ else: 102→ # Fallback to flat list for releases without categories 103→ for item_text, indent_level in release.items: 104→ indent = " " + (" " * indent_level) 105→ click.echo(f"{indent}• {item_text}", file=sys.stderr) 106→ click.echo(file=sys.stderr) 107→ 108→ click.echo(click.style(" " + "─" * 50, dim=True), file=sys.stderr) 109→ click.echo(file=sys.stderr) 110→ 111→ # Prompt user to continue (only if stdin is a TTY) 112→ console = InteractiveConsole() 113→ if console.is_stdin_interactive(): 114→ click.pause(info=click.style(" Press Enter to continue...", dim=True), err=True) 115→ except click.Abort: 116→ # User pressed Ctrl+C or declined - exit gracefully 117→ raise SystemExit(0) from None 118→ except Exception as e: 119→ # Never let release notes break the CLI, but warn so issues can be diagnosed 120→ logging.warning("Failed to show version change banner: %s", e) 121→ 122→ 123→def _show_version_warning() -> None: 124→ """Show warning if installed erk version doesn't match repo-required version. 125→ 126→ This is designed to never fail - exceptions are logged but don't break the CLI. 127→ """ 128→ # Skip if user has disabled version checking 129→ if os.environ.get("ERK_SKIP_VERSION_CHECK") == "1":

500→ # Write back to file 501→ with pyproject_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f: 502→ tomlkit.dump(doc, f) 503→ 504→ 505→def safe_cwd() -> tuple[Path | None, str | None]: 506→ """Get current working directory, detecting if it no longer exists. 507→ 508→ Uses LBYL approach: checks if the operation will succeed before attempting it. 509→ 510→ Returns: 511→ tuple[Path | None, str | None]: (path, error_message) 512→ - If successful: (Path, None) 513→ - If directory deleted: (None, error_message) 514→ 515→ Note: 516→ This is an acceptable use of try/except since we're wrapping a third-party 517→ API (Path.cwd()) that provides no way to check the condition first. 518→ """ 519→ try: 520→ cwd_path = Path.cwd() 521→ return (cwd_path, None) 522→ except (FileNotFoundError, OSError): 523→ return ( 524→ None, 525→ "Current working directory no longer exists", 526→ ) 527→ 528→ 529→def create_context(*, dry_run: bool, script: bool = False, debug: bool = False) -> ErkContext:

1→"""Dispatch GitHub Actions workflows via unified interface. 2→ 3→Usage examples: 4→ erk launch pr-address --pr 456 5→ erk launch pr-rebase --pr 456 6→ erk launch pr-rebase --pr 456 --no-squash 7→ erk launch plan-implement --issue 789 8→ erk launch learn --issue 789 9→ erk launch one-shot --pr 456 --prompt "fix the auth bug" 10→ erk launch one-shot --pr 456 -f prompt.md 11→ erk launch pr-rebase --pr 456 --repo owner/repo # remote mode 12→""" 13→ 14→from pathlib import Path 15→ 16→import click 17→ 18→from erk.cli.commands.pr.metadata_helpers import maybe_update_plan_dispatch_metadata 19→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 20→from erk.cli.constants import WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP 21→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 22→from erk.cli.repo_resolution import get_remote_github, repo_option, resolve_owner_repo 23→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 24→from erk.core.repo_discovery import NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 26→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 27→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.types import RemotePRNotFound 28→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 29→ 30→

550→ owner=owner, 551→ repo_name=repo_name, 552→ workflow_name="learn", 553→ inputs=inputs, 554→ ref=ref, 555→ ) 556→ 557→ 558→def _dispatch_one_shot_remote( 559→ remote: RemoteGitHub, 560→ *, 561→ owner: str, 562→ repo_name: str, 563→ pr_number: int, 564→ prompt: str, 565→ model: str | None, 566→ ref: str | None, 567→) -> None: 568→ """Dispatch one-shot workflow in remote mode.""" 569→ user_output("Checking PR status...") 570→ pr = remote.get_pr(owner=owner, repo=repo_name, number=pr_number) 571→ Ensure.invariant( 572→ not isinstance(pr, RemotePRNotFound), 573→ f"No pull request found with number #{pr_number}", 574→ ) 575→ assert not isinstance(pr, RemotePRNotFound) 576→ 577→ Ensure.invariant( 578→ pr.state == "OPEN", 579→ f"Cannot run one-shot on {pr.state} PR - only OPEN PRs can be targeted",

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have the complete understanding. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:
Based on my analysis of the erk codebase, here's the complete picture of how the `@no_repo_required` pattern works:
## How `@no_repo_required` Works in Erk
### 1. **No Explicit Decorator - It's a Pattern, Not a Decorator**
There is **no `@no_repo_required` decorator**. Instead, the pattern is implemented through: - A `NoRepoSentinel` marker class that replaces the real `RepoContext` when no repo is found - Commands checking `isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)` to decide between local and remote modes - Conditional routing to different handler functions
### 2. **The Key Classes and Components**
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/core/context.py`** - Line 35: `NoRepoSentinel` is re-exported from `erk_shared.context.types` - Line 597: In `create_context()`, the function calls `discover_repo_or_sentinel(cwd, erk_root, git)` which returns either a `RepoContext` or `NoRepoSentinel()`
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py`** - `repo_option` (line 75): Click option decorator for the `--repo owner/repo` flag - `resolve_owner_repo()` (line 15): Function to resolve owner/repo from either `--repo` flag or local git context - `get_remote_github()` (line 49): Factory function to get or construct a `RemoteGitHub`
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py`** - Line 767: Uses `@repo_option` decorator to add the `--repo` flag - Line 830: The routing logic: `is_remote = target_repo is not None or isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)`
### 3. **Context Creation: Where NoRepoSentinel Gets Injected**
The process happens in `create_context()` at `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/core/context.py`:
``` Lines 594-597: repo = discover_repo_or_sentinel(cwd, erk_root, git) # Returns NoRepoSentinel() if no .git directory is found ```
Then at lines 620-635: ```python if isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel): local_config = LoadedConfig.test() # Use test defaults else: # Load real config from repo ```
### 4. **The Launch Command Pattern (Real Implementation)**
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py`**
The complete pattern (lines 716-847):
```python @click.command("launch") @click.argument("workflow_name", type=str) # ... many @click.option decorators ... @repo_option # ← Adds --repo flag @click.pass_obj def launch( ctx: ErkContext, workflow_name: str, *, # ... many parameters ... target_repo: str | None, # ← From @repo_option ) -> None: """Dispatch a GitHub Actions workflow."""
# Line 830: Routing logic is_remote = target_repo is not None or isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)
if is_remote: # Line 836-847: Call remote handler if ref_current: raise click.UsageError("--ref-current cannot be used in remote mode") _launch_remote(ctx, target_repo=target_repo, workflow_name=workflow_name, ...) return
# Line 851: Assert we have a real repo for local mode assert not isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) repo: RepoContext = ctx.repo # ... local mode handling ... ```
### 5. **Remote Mode Handlers**
The `_launch_remote()` function (lines 608-714) demonstrates the pattern:
```python def _launch_remote( ctx: ErkContext, *, target_repo: str | None, workflow_name: str, ... ) -> None: """Handle launch command in remote mode (--repo flag or no local repo).""" # Line 622: Resolve owner/repo from either --repo flag or local context owner, repo_name = resolve_owner_repo(ctx, target_repo=target_repo) # Line 623: Get RemoteGitHub instance remote = get_remote_github(ctx)
# Route to workflow-specific remote handlers if workflow_name == "pr-rebase": _dispatch_pr_rebase_remote(remote, owner=owner, repo_name=repo_name, ...) elif workflow_name == "pr-address": _dispatch_pr_address_remote(remote, owner=owner, repo_name=repo_name, ...) # ... etc ```
### 6. **The NoRepoSentinel Guard Pattern**
From `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` (lines 41-45):
```python if isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) or ctx.repo.github is None: raise UserFacingCliError( "Cannot determine target repository.\n" "Use --repo owner/repo or run from inside a git repository." ) ```
### 7. **How One-Shot Uses the Pattern**
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py`**
The `one_shot` command uses the exact same pattern: - Line 26: Imports `NoRepoSentinel` - Lines 32-55: Factory function `_get_remote_github()` that gets or constructs `RemoteGitHub` - Similar logic to check if in remote mode and route accordingly
### Summary: The Pattern vs. Decorator
The "**@no_repo_required**" terminology is a **conceptual pattern**, not an actual Python decorator:
1. **Detection**: Commands check `isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)` to detect no-repo mode 2. **Registration**: No explicit list - each command that supports it implements the check 3. **Marker**: `NoRepoSentinel` is the sentinel object that marks "no repo found" 4. **Context injection**: During `create_context()`, `discover_repo_or_sentinel()` injects the sentinel 5. **Routing**: Commands use conditional logic to route to either local or remote handlers 6. **Infrastructure**: - `@repo_option` decorator adds the `--repo` flag - `resolve_owner_repo()` resolves from flag or local context - `get_remote_github()` factory constructs the remote interface - Remote handlers use `RemoteGitHub` ABC methods instead of local `GitHub` methods
The pattern is **not a decorator** but rather a **conditional architecture** where commands that need to work without a repo simply check for `NoRepoSentinel` and conditionally route to remote-capable implementations.
